Renewable energy is referred to as energy obtained from virtually inexhaustible natural sources, either because of the immense amount of energy they contain, or because they are able to regenerate by natural means for the moderation of the mining of fluids, gases and fossils. Renewable energies include wind energy, geothermal energy, hydroelectric energy, tidal energy, solar energy, undimotive energy, biomass and biofuels.
At present, they are becoming more important because of the worsening greenhouse effect and the consequent global warming, accompanied by increased international awareness of the problem.
In short, it contributes to the climate stability of the planet.
